_report_metadata.title: LinkedIn Pipeline Error Analysis — for cross-LLM review
_report_metadata.purpose: Structured error data for a second LLM model to cross-analyze failures, classify root causes, and recommend fixes.

navigation_timeout_15s.observed_pattern: All 17 failures are identical: goto() on job view URL times out at exactly 15s. Tried once, no retry (timeout is non-retryable per _NON_RETRYABLE_PATTERNS). Duration per job: ~15.0s.
navigation_timeout_15s.downstream_impact: Pool page (3 pages) blocked for 15s each. Only 22 of 50 jobs were processed before pipeline wrapped up in 241s.

job_timeout_120s.observed_pattern: These jobs were queued (waiting on semaphore) behind the 17 failing jobs. By the time they acquired a pool page, the browser had already been processing for 120s, triggering asyncio.wait_for timeout. Duration per job: exactly 120.0s.
job_timeout_120s.downstream_impact: False failures — these jobs never even attempted goto(). They are collateral damage from the 17 timeout failures blocking the pool.

root_cause_analysis.primary_hypothesis: LinkedIn rate-limiting or temporary slowness on job view pages (/jobs/view/NNNN)
evidence: Tracker page (/jobs-tracker/) loads fine (~25-33s for 5 pages with pagination)
Job view pages consistently timeout at 15s — ALL of them, 100% failure
Previous run at 18:00 had 50/50 success; subsequent runs at 19:00+ all fail
Auth check passes (10.5s) — session is valid, cookies intact
Resource blocking active (images/fonts/media blocked) — not a bandwidth issue
root_cause_analysis.alternative_hypothesis: Chromium page pool resource exhaustion after repeated navigations
evidence: Patten: jobs 1-2 succeed, then ALL subsequent jobs fail with timeout
Could indicate session/connection pool exhaustion in Chromium
recommended_debug: Run pipeline sequentially (parallel=False) to eliminate pool/page reuse as variable
Increase per_job_timeout_ms to 30000 to test if LinkedIn is just slow vs truly blocked
Add page.goto() response status code logging to detect 429/503 from LinkedIn
Check LinkedIn session health: can a manually navigated browser access job view pages?
Add exponential backoff between retries at pipeline level (not just per-goto)

successful_jobs.title: Product Owner – AI, Automation & Tooling for IT Service Desk

known_issues_from_pitevni_kniha: P5: LinkedIn SPA selector drift — 'Specific job list selector not found' observed in every run (benign, fallback to general works)
P8: Auth cache staleness when pipeline exceeds 60s per-job phase — fixed by SESSION_CHECK_INTERVAL 60s→300s (commit f3a5048). Applies here: per_job_phase=120s, auth cache still valid.
P12: Pool page round-robin + semaphore mismatch — MAX_POOL_SIZE was 5 vs MAX_CONCURRENT=3, now both=3 (commit bd5b5dc). Still 29 jobs timed out at 120s due to pool exhaustion cascade.
P15: TargetClosedError during browser close — reduced via _drain_pending_navs(3s) in close_browser (commit bd5b5dc). Still 0 occurrences in this run (fixed).

mcp_jobs_comparison.architecture: MCP-Jobs uses requests+BS4 (~1s/page), no browser. LinkedIn pipeline uses Patchright browser (~4-15s/page).
mcp_jobs_comparison.rate_limiting: MCP-Jobs respects robots.txt + polite delays. LinkedIn has no public API; browser-based scraping is inherently rate-limited.
mcp_jobs_comparison.success_rate_benchmark: MCP-Jobs: consistent ~90-100% page fetch rate. LinkedIn pipeline: 50/50 normal, 4/50 under rate-limit.

recommendations.action: Sequential fallback mode: when >50% of parallel jobs fail with timeout, automatically retry sequentially
recommendations.rationale: Pool exhaustion cascade kills rest of pipeline. Sequential mode would process remaining jobs one-by-one with longer timeout.

recommendations.action: Log HTTP response status from page.goto() resp argument
recommendations.rationale: Currently resp.status is only checked for >=400. Logging 200/301/429 would reveal if LinkedIn returns non-standard responses (e.g., 429 with body that doesn't match is_rate_limited()).

recommendations.action: Add progressive timeout: start with 15s, on consecutive failures increase to 30s, then give up
recommendations.rationale: Adaptive timeout avoids both false positives (15s too short for slow LinkedIn) and pool blockage (30s too long when LinkedIn is healthy).

recommendations.action: Add exponential pipeline-level backoff: if N consecutive jobs fail with timeout, pause 30s before continuing
recommendations.rationale: Currently no pipeline-level rate limiting. LinkedIn may be throttling because we send 3 parallel requests. Backoff would reduce pressure.

recommendations.action: Run diagnostic: manual browser navigation to job view URLs to confirm if LinkedIn blocks headless Chromium
recommendations.rationale: Could be that LinkedIn upgraded bot detection and blocks Patchright specifically. Manual test would confirm.
